title: knema tridactyla subsp. tridactyla
intro: Knema tridactyla subsp. tridactyla, also known as the Three-fingered Knema, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique three-fingered leaves. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Knema tridactyla subsp. tridactyla include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
